name: Rempah (Malay Curry)
recipeIngredient: 12 ounces White Yam, peeled and cubed 1 inch
recipeInstructions:
1. Peel and cut sweet potato and white yam into 1-inch cubes.
2. Make a paste of the spices by combining them with enough water to coat all ingredients. Let stand for a few minutes.
3. Puree onion, garlic, chillies, red pepper, and ginger separately.
4. Heat oil in a large pan and fry spice paste for about 5 minutes, stirring frequently to prevent burning.
5. Add the pureed vegetables and cook for an additional 15 minutes.
6. Combine cubed sweet potato and yam with the spice mixture in a casserole dish.
7. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and place the casserole in the oven.
8. Cook for about 1 hour, stirring occasionally.
9. In a separate pan, heat milk and melt creamed coconut.
10. Brown dessicated coconut under grill.
11. About halfway through cooking, add the two coconuts and tamarind puree to the dish. Stir well and add water or vegetable stock if needed for desired consistency.
12. The finished curry should have a thick, rich sauce.
13. Serve 4.
